author: @donaldburkhard7932
text: Could you use magnetic bearings as secondary use to create the power with coils?

author: @jbay088
text:
If I were making it low cost, I wouldn't put moving magnets on the track. It would be much easier to use a belt or chain, like you do, and then just pick off power at any point via sprockets or pulleys, to a rotary generator placed along the perimeter. Better power density, lower cost, and with the benefits of a gear ratio.
Anyway, great video and quite amazing that you put together a demo so quickly!

author: @willhibbardii2450
text: Another good one Mate. Fun to daydream... I love sailing and flying. Full circle circuit sailing or an aircraft holding pattern.  In my mind's eye the layout instead of being a rectangular course could be maximized probably by the track being laid out like a wing to the dominant prevailing winds. Depending on scale perhaps could be a grid system with multipurpose application. Generating power, Sightseeing, Transportation, Housing, Dining and many spinoffs... Architectural engineers should have fun designing possibilities around this concept. Monorails were promising as were sky lifts however never manifested except in amusement parks. My favorite is the linear motion track transportation system. Many walls, fences and present existing architectures to upgrade.

author: @kadmow
text:
Football / sports stadiums and racetracks may be ideal locations for such a generating device...

 Zone variable AoA control using an elevator/tab is very possible  (operation,  power / comms - sliprings / brushes  or solar panel with short range radio for onboard actuuation electronics / control, vs a cam follower on the  (second movable "control"rail - while easy to set up, les tech on each "blade unit",  it is more difficult to vary for demand following)  - allowing for variable wind conditions around the track.

(NB, in case any aren't sailers or kite fliers -  that the reverse reach needs a reversed Angle of attack for the wing (blade) - achievable in several ways - trimtab reversal (non-optimal symmetrical aerofoil),  or  for the blade unit to rotate to inverted for the reverse run (unidirectional optimised aerofoil)... -   the reach to tack leg needs to be as streamlined as possible (Zero AOA).

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a good point mate

replies.author: @donnievance1942
replies.text: I don't see any intrinsic scale limits. However, it seems to me that one of the strengths of this concept is that it lends itself to decentralized and dispersed multiple installations with lower barriers to capital formation. Many relatively small such installations are likely to be both more financially and physically efficient that fewer, larger ones.

author: @blueredbrick
text:
I might as well mention the video from youtuber Electric Viking from yesterday in case you missed it about the same topic of wind energy : "Bill Gates backed game changing wind energy device reduces cost by 65%". at the 4:30 mark he goes into the details of that Airloom system as well. But more the merrier in this field because we're long overdue for the transition. 
What we need more than ever is affordable and safe storage of electrical energy to lessen the load on overstressed grids while also empowering ppl with energy independance.
I see it everyday with my parents donating tens to hundred euro worth of electricity (depending on the season) to the grid while still getting billed at the end of the year because they practically got nothing in return per kWh and pay through the nose for bought kWh's and also being billed for using the net both ways. Storage is a real issue and it is hard to prove the importance of it personally to them because I'm not financially capable of just go out and buying a powerwall or any other from another brand and plainly demontrate it. 'Hydrogen economy is still a thing in their mind" and I choose not discussing about the topic in depth in the family since years because it's usually seen as arguing rather then discussing. That's combined with not yet openly available existance legislation on energy distribution here in Nl. There are some initiatives but it so slow. In the meaintime I can use all the electricity I want or need during the day but when the sun goes down I'll just use my laptop and powerbanks that I recharged during the day. But it's cumbersome. Add to that the want or need for an electric vehicle. But I enjoyed your video as always Robert, have a good night.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: My silly question is, does mass wind-energy harvesting not remove joules from weather patterns, thereby as a whole, altering the weather patterns it is supposed to be a defender of? Just my half-wit two cents - recall I have no scientific training. I mean maybe making more complex weather patterns, but is the removal of joules from wind currents in any way detrimental to existent weather patterns, even from  microclimatic perspective? Adding another jumbler into Pandora's weatherbox? I really do wonder. I would also say that I would want a device reliant not on the external environment during operation but rather material properties and design, once constructed, rather than having a further impact on the environment whatsoever other than electrical output during its operation.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the only way to have no effect is to do nothing mate

replies.author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering lol  guess. I meant once its designed it has some inherent propety that only outputs raw electricity nd nothing else, post production. I agree with you though, lol. I'm a bit ideoilllogical  I guess

replies.author: @ministerulmagiei
replies.text: why for cars? they should be safe by heating the air because it is drying it, and when it it condensating on the glass/windscreen it should only be recirculating the air inside and heating that one

replies.author: @ministerulmagiei
replies.text: I have a cheap and lightweight design for atmospheric water generator (dehumidifier) for off grid transport and apocalyptic scenarios, ping me if you want more details

replies.author: @simonexperiences
replies.text:
@ministerulmagiei 
because i live in very moist country, and if i don't run every day, every thing is spongious....(sorry for my english)

replies.author: @ministerulmagiei
replies.text: @simonexperiences I think I know how to prevent it, so you need to guess the temperature of the weather in the future, if you know you will start the car in the morning on 17 November, and there will be 2 degrees C outside, you need to lower the air temperature inside the car on the night before to 10 degrees for example. When you switch to cooling the air and recirculating it, the water will start to condense, a pool of water will form under your car, and that is the water you would've had on your windows if you didn't do it. You will get colder air in the car, the car will be drier, and even if during the night the temperature will drop to 2 C, water will not condense as much, because you took it out previously the night before.

author: @cosmicseekin
text: No one is going to believe me if I tell you I had this exact concept 10 years ago.  I thought aircraft wings on an elongated track with auto pitch would be a great step forward from VAWTs with a single central shaft.  I even thought of each wing carrying its own magnets  and using MAGLEV to eliminate friction on the runners.  The power from each wing being transmitted through  the tracks.  It could be a bit like a ski lift where wings could be swapped in and out for repair or tuning to the wind conditions.   Way beyond my technical ability to put all that technology together.  An after thought was to have a big track and have kites on long lines running round the track.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is a good idea mate

replies.author: @hunakosdem
replies.text: I believe you because I had this exact same idea in my mind since at least 10 years ago. Including the final thoughts of Robert at the end of the video.

replies.author: @anthonypape6862
replies.text: @donnievance1942 Can you patent basic principles of physics though?  The invention comes from Tesla, everyone is just thinking of ways to get a magnet to pass quickly by a coil of copper wire using other inventions like flywheels, pulleys, wind turbines, any property of physics, planet gears, axial flux, some alone some together.  That's whats cool about Robert's channel. All of these ideas, and he's just having fun building.  I don't think there is any money it.  All we need is invented already.  Will we build it or will we make a great mistake and ignore the problem.
